title: data weave sample module master
description: This project is a DataWeave module, specifically a reusable library containing common string manipulation functions. It extends DataWeave's built-in capabilities, allowing Mule applications to import and utilize these custom functions (like repeating a string). The module is packaged for deployment to Anypoint Exchange, facilitating reuse across multiple projects. It exemplifies creating modular, testable DataWeave code.

bestPractices.title: DataWeave Module Documentation
bestPractices.reason: The `StringUtils.dwl` file includes Javadoc-style comments explaining the module's purpose and the `times` function.

bestPractices.title: DataWeave Unit Tests
bestPractices.reason: Comprehensive unit tests are provided in `StringUtilsTest.dwl` using `dw::test` to validate the `times` function's behavior.

bestPractices.title: Clear Function Signatures
bestPractices.reason: The `times` function in `StringUtils.dwl` uses explicit type annotations for its parameters (`value: String`, `amount: Number`), improving readability and maintainability.

bestPractices.title: Hardcoded Secrets / External Connectivity
bestPractices.reason: As a pure DataWeave utility module, it does not connect to external systems or contain any hardcoded credentials, making this check irrelevant and passing by design.

documentation:
# Project Guide

## Overview
This project defines a DataWeave module named `string-utils-weave-module` which provides custom string utility functions. The primary function `times` allows repeating a given string a specified number of times. This module is designed to be published to Anypoint Exchange and consumed by other Mule applications as a reusable DataWeave library, enhancing code modularity and reusability. It includes unit tests to ensure the correctness of the implemented functions.

## Module Structure
- `pom.xml`: Defines project metadata, dependencies (DataWeave runtime, testing framework), and build plugins (weave-maven-plugin for testing and deployment to Exchange).
- `src/main/resources/org/mule/commons/StringUtils.dwl`: Contains the core DataWeave functions, including `times` for string repetition. It is commented for clarity.
- `src/test/dwtest/org/mule/commons/StringUtilsTest.dwl`: Provides unit tests for the `StringUtils` module, verifying the `times` function's behavior under various conditions (e.g., positive repetitions, zero repetitions).

cards.title: String Repetition Function (`times`)
cards.description: The `times` function takes a `String` value and a `Number` amount. It repeatedly concatenates the `value` to itself `amount` times. If `amount` is 0 or less, it returns an empty string. This function demonstrates basic DataWeave looping and string manipulation, packaged for reusability.

cards.title: Module Packaging & Testing
cards.description: The project uses Maven to package the DataWeave module. The `weave-maven-plugin` is configured to execute DataWeave unit tests (`StringUtilsTest.dwl`) during the `test` phase, ensuring the module's functions work as expected before deployment. Distribution to Anypoint Exchange is configured via `distributionManagement`.
